tiers.label: Let's get you set up
tiers.intro: Teaches: voice/text capture, inbox, how Fulkit files things, first integration.
tiers.trust_line: Everything you tell me stays in your files unless you decide otherwise. I don't build a profile on you — you're building one for yourself. I just read what you let me read. That's the deal.
tiers.assignment_copy: I just filed what you told me into your inbox. Go take a look — you'll see how I organize things so you don't have to.

questions.text: Before we start — what brought you here? Just curious.
questions.why: No wrong answer. Just helps me understand what you're looking for.

questions.text: What should I call you?
questions.why: Creates identity file. Everything starts with a name.

questions.text: Where are you based?
questions.why: Time zones, local context, recommendations.

questions.text: What do you do?
questions.why: Shapes whether Fulkit thinks in terms of bosses, clients, customers, or professors.

questions.text: Quick preference — do you like being asked questions, or would you rather I just figure things out on my own?
questions.why: Shapes how I interact with you going forward.
options: Ask me — I like being involved
Figure it out — just show me results

questions.text: What are you working on right now? Give me two or three things — messy is fine.
questions.why: This is the first real capture. Teaches you the product works.
questions.trust_line: Whatever you say here becomes a note in YOUR files. Not mine. Yours. Delete it anytime and it's gone — I don't keep a copy.

questions.text: Connect something so I can show you what I do.
questions.why: Unlocks a live feature demo.
questions.copy_after_answer: Nice. You just unlocked something. Go check it out when you're ready.

tiers.label: How your brain works
tiers.intro: Teaches: search, the filing system, threads, Context Control settings.
tiers.trust_line: Quick reminder — this is burn-after-reading technology. Everything lives locally in your files. I process what you share with me to be helpful, then the conversation is done. I'm not building a dossier. You are building a brain — and you hold every key to it.
tiers.assignment_copy: Try searching for one of the things you told me in Tier 1. Just a word or two. See what comes back. Also check your task list — I set something up based on what keeps falling through your cracks.

questions.text: Real talk — how do you feel about AI knowing your stuff?
questions.why: There's no wrong answer. This shapes how I handle your data.
options: A little weird but worth it
I'm cautious — show me the controls
questions.trust_line: Honest answer only. There's no wrong one — and it changes how I handle your stuff.

questions.text: When you save something, how do you usually try to find it later?
questions.why: Shapes how Fulkit prioritizes search results.
options: I search for what it was about
I try to remember when I saved it
I scroll until I see it

questions.text: What keeps falling through the cracks?
questions.why: Tells Fulkit where to focus whispers and what to watch for.

questions.text: Here's where your stuff lives.
questions.why: This is your file system. I built it, I maintain it, and I file things here for you automatically. You don't need to organize anything — that's my job. You've got four ways to see it: a Board for dragging things around, a List for scanning, a Calendar for due dates, and a Table for details.
questions.trust_line: Every file here is yours. It's all markdown — plain text you can open anywhere. Export it, delete it, take it somewhere else. No lock-in.

questions.text: Go check your Context Control.
questions.why: This is how you control what I can see. You can see exactly what I know about you and dial each thing up or down.
questions.trust_line: Most apps don't let you see what they know about you, let alone turn it off. This is yours to control. Always.

questions.text: Your data lives in one of three places — your device only, encrypted in the cloud, or on Fülkit's servers. Want to pick now or decide later?
questions.why: This is your vault. It controls where your files are stored and who can access them.

tiers.label: What matters to you
tiers.intro: Teaches: calendar integration, reminders, personal/work coexistence, whispers.
tiers.trust_line: I'm about to ask some personal stuff. Not because I need it — because the more I understand what your actual life looks like, the better I don't let things slip. Skip anything. The skips tell me something too. And same deal: this all lives in your files. Not ours. Yours.
tiers.assignment_copy: If you told me a goal, check your calendar — I put a check-in there. If you told me what you're into, go look at your threads — it has its own space now.

questions.text: Anything you want to make sure I never bring up? Certain topics, people, situations — some things are better left alone.
questions.why: If you tell me, I won't go there. Ever.
questions.trust_line: This one matters. If you tell me, I won't go there. Ever.

questions.text: Outside of work, what are you into right now?
questions.why: Seeds personal context so Fulkit isn't a work-only tool.
questions.copy_after_answer: I just gave that its own space in your brain. Drop stuff in there anytime — I'll keep it separate from work.

questions.text: Is there something you're working toward this year?
questions.why: If you answer, Fulkit creates a milestone note with a gentle recurring check-in.
questions.placeholder: A goal, a milestone, a target...
questions.copy_after_answer: Check your calendar. I left something there for you.

questions.text: Who comes up a lot? Just first names — so I know who 'Mike' is when you mention him.
questions.why: Context seeding. Not a contacts list — just names Fulkit can recognize.
questions.trust_line: I'm not storing contacts or phone numbers. Just names so I don't ask 'who's Mike?' every time you mention him. Delete any of these from your files anytime.

questions.text: How often do you want me checking in?
questions.why: Sets whisper frequency from day one.
options: Surprise me — I'll tell you if it's too much

questions.text: How's this going so far? Too personal? Not personal enough? Just right?
questions.why: Calibrates the rest of your onboarding.

tiers.label: Show me how you work
tiers.intro: Teaches: quick capture, whisper behavior, the action list, the write-back loop.
tiers.trust_line: Almost dialed in. These last few shape how we actually work together day to day. Same rules — your files, your device, your call. Everything I learn here makes me faster at helping you. Nothing leaves unless you send it.
tiers.assignment_copy: Try capturing something right now. Say it out loud or type it — whatever you picked. Watch where it goes. Also check your action list — I built it from everything you've told me so far.

questions.text: How do you capture ideas right now?
questions.why: Determines whether Fulkit pushes voice capture, quick text, or meets you where you are.

questions.text: Are you more of a morning person or night owl?
questions.why: Determines when whispers arrive and when Fulkit checks in.

questions.text: Want a morning briefing? Scores, news, weather — or just your stuff?
questions.why: Tells Fulkit whether to be a morning dashboard or stay focused on the brain.

questions.text: Here's what your morning could look like.
questions.why: This is your bestie briefing — a personalized snapshot assembled from everything you've told me so far.

questions.text: What's one thing you wish an app could do for you that none of them do?
questions.why: This is the gold. Tells Fulkit what magic moment to create for you specifically.

questions.text: Compact or comfortable? Some people want the sidebar tight and minimal. Others want labels on everything.
questions.why: Sets your sidebar preference right now.

tiers.label: The real stuff
tiers.intro: The full power of a second brain that knows you. This is the 'holy shit' tier.
tiers.trust_line: You've been here a minute now. I know how you think, what you're working on, and what you care about. This last round isn't about setup — it's about letting me actually help. Your files. Your brain. I'm just the one paying attention.
tiers.assignment_copy: Go check your action list. Go check your threads. Look at your calendar. Everything you've told me across all five tiers is in there — organized, connected, and working for you. This is your brain now.

questions.text: What's the thing you keep meaning to get to but never do?
questions.why: Fulkit breaks it into a first step and puts it on the action list.
questions.placeholder: That thing you keep putting off...
questions.copy_after_answer: Check your tasks. I didn't plan the whole thing — just the first move.

questions.text: What stresses you out most right now?
questions.why: Fulkit becomes aware without adding pressure. Won't pile on in these areas.
questions.trust_line: I'm not a therapist and I won't pretend to be. But if money stresses you out, I'm not going to send you a whisper about your budget at 11pm. Context matters.

questions.text: Your brain can talk to other tools. Here's a taste of what's possible.
questions.why: These are integrations you can connect anytime from Settings. No setup needed to browse — just see what's possible.

questions.text: What does a really good day look like for you?
questions.why: This is Fulkit's north star. Shapes long-term whisper direction.

questions.text: If I could magically handle one recurring annoyance in your life, what would it be?
questions.why: Fulkit proposes an automation or recurring workflow based on this.
questions.placeholder: That thing that keeps bugging you...

tiers.label: Power Tools
tiers.intro: Now that you're dialed in, here's the full toolkit.
tiers.trust_line: These are power features. They're optional — but once you see them, you'll want them.
tiers.assignment_copy: Your power tools are set up. Go explore.

questions.text: Want unlimited messages? Plug in your own Anthropic API key and the meter disappears.
questions.why: If you have a Claude API key, you can use it here for unlimited conversations.
options: I have a key — let's do it
I'm good with what I've got
questions.copy_after_answer: Head to Settings → Privacy to plug it in. Once it's connected, you're unlimited.

questions.text: Got files you want me to know about? Drag them in or paste text — I'll read it all.
questions.why: Your vault can ingest markdown, text, and notes from anywhere.

questions.text: Share your link — you and a friend both get extra credits.
questions.why: Referrals earn you and your friend bonus messages every month.

tiers.label: Your Integrations
tiers.intro: Your brain can connect to the tools you already use.
tiers.trust_line: Every integration is optional. Connect what makes sense. Disconnect anytime.
tiers.assignment_copy: Check your connected tools. See what you can ask me now.

questions.text: Here's what your connected tools can do.
questions.why: Each integration unlocks new commands. Here's a preview of what you can ask.

questions.text: Want to connect something new?
questions.why: Browse available integrations and connect the ones that make sense for you.

tiers.label: Make It Yours
tiers.intro: Final touches. Make this space feel like yours.
tiers.trust_line: You own everything here. Every file, every note, every setting. Take it all with you if you ever leave.
tiers.assignment_copy: Your workspace is ready. Everything is organized, connected, and yours.

questions.text: Your threads have custom folders and views. Want to set up your workspace?
questions.why: Create folders for your projects, switch between Board, List, Calendar, and Table views.

questions.text: Your data is yours. You can export everything anytime.
questions.why: No lock-in. Settings → Privacy → Export gives you all your data in plain text.
